title: OMV Builds One of the Largest Electrolysis Plants for Green Hydrogen in Europe
content:
OMV has begun construction of a 140 MW green hydrogen electrolysis plant in Bruck an der Leitha, Austria, set to be one of the largest in Europe and the biggest in Austria and South-Eastern Europe. Scheduled to begin operations by late 2027, the plant will produce up to 23,000 tons of hydrogen annually using renewable power from wind, solar, and hydropower. This is expected to reduce carbon emissions from OMVâ€™s Schwechat refinery by up to 150,000 tons per year, equivalent to about 10% of its current direct emissions.

The project, involving an investment in the mid-hundreds of millions of euros, includes a 22-kilometer hydrogen pipeline linking the new facility to the refinery. Backed by the European Hydrogen Bank and awaiting final funding approval, the project strengthens OMVâ€™s position in refinery decarbonization and builds on its existing 10 MW electrolyzer in Austria. Siemens Energy and STRABAG will lead implementation under an EPC contract, combining electrolysis technology, plant construction, and civil engineering expertise. OMV positions the project as a milestone in its Strategy 2030 energy transition roadmap.
author: Analysts Account
